content: IP allowlisting in Next.js middleware: read x-forwarded-for (first comma-split value) or req.ip. Vercel rewrites X-Forwarded-For so plain-text is reliable on Vercel non-Enterprise (Enterprise allows trusted proxy custom headers). For CIDR matching use netmask npm package (contains() method) or cidr-matcher 2.1.1 (IPv4/IPv6, fast, last updated 7 years ago but stable). Store allowlist in env var (comma-separated CIDRs) or DB. Return NextResponse 403 for blocked IPs. Run at middleware before auth check. Also consider Vercel's platform-level CIDR blocking (Enterprise feature) as alternative.
